drugs.notes: Cycles 1-8: days 1, 4, 8, 11. Cycles 9-16: days 1, 8 only. Dose rounded to agreed bands. At least 72 hours must elapse between doses. Intravenous injection.

dose_modifications.condition: Panobinostat hepatic impairment - bilirubin 1-1.5xULN and/or abnormal AST
dose_modifications.notes: Start at reduced dose of 15mg in first cycle and increase as tolerated. If occurs during treatment, temporarily discontinue until grade 2 or less, then restart reduced by one dose level.

dose_modifications.condition: Panobinostat hepatic impairment - bilirubin 1.5-3xULN and/or abnormal AST
dose_modifications.notes: Start at 10mg in first cycle and increase to 15mg as tolerated. If occurs during treatment, temporarily discontinue until grade 2 or less, then restart reduced by one dose level.
100% of dose
Clinical decision required

dose_modifications.condition: Panobinostat renal impairment - mild to moderate
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustments necessary in mild to moderate renal impairment

dose_modifications.condition: Bortezomib peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 1 with pain or grade 2

dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue until resolved to grade 1 or less, then reinitiate at 0.7mg/m²

dose_modifications.condition: Bortezomib peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 4 or severe autonomic neuropathy
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue bortezomib permanently

Omit dose until recovery to grade 1 or less then resume at reduced dose level or change to once weekly
Omit dose until recovery to grade 1 or less then resume at reduced dose
Omit dose until recovery to grade 1 or less then resume at same dose on once weekly schedule
Permanently discontinue
Permanently discontinue

dose_modifications.condition: Panobinostat QTcF prolongation >=480ms or >60ms from baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Omit dose. If resolved within 7 days, resume at prior dose (initial) or reduced dose (recurrent). If unresolved within 7 days, discontinue. If QTcF >500ms, permanently discontinue.

pre_medications: Cyclizine 50mg up to three times a day oral when required (take home anti-emetic)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral
Allopurinol 300mg once a day for 7 days oral (cycle 1 only)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2mg after each loose stool to maximum 16mg/24 hours oral
Gastric protection: proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist in high-risk patients
Bisphosphonates in accordance with local policies
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
special_warnings: Panobinostat prolongs the QT interval. ECG required prior to day 1; QTcF must be <480ms before initiating panobinostat. Repeat ECG on day 1 of cycle 2. Agents also associated with QT prolongation (including ondansetron, metoclopramide, domperidone) should be used with caution.
Subcutaneous administration of bortezomib should be considered to reduce peripheral neuropathy risk.
Oral chemotherapy - NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. This is a short course oral chemotherapy that must not be continued.
Patients must be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test at screening; barrier contraception required.

FBC should be checked prior to each dose of bortezomib (days 1 and 8 in cycles 9-16; days 1 and 8, with consideration before days 15 and/or 18 in patients >=65 years or platelets <150x10^9/L in cycles 1-8).
LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle. ECG prior to day 1.
All dose reductions due to adverse reactions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval. If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Bortezomib dose rounded to agreed bands. At least 72 hours must elapse between bortezomib doses.

Omit dose until recovery to grade <=1 then resume at reduced dose level or change to once weekly
Omit dose until recovery to grade <=1 then resume at reduced dose
Omit dose until recovery to grade <=1 then resume at same dose on once weekly schedule
Permanently discontinue
Permanently discontinue

dose_modifications.condition: Panobinostat QTcF prolongation >=480ms or >60ms from baseline
dose_modifications.notes: Omit dose. If resolved within 7 days, resume at prior dose (initial) or reduced (recurrent). If unresolved within 7 days, discontinue. If QTcF >500ms, permanently discontinue.
May stop; can re-introduce at 50mg with escalation at subsequent cycles to 100mg if good anticoagulant control and no other side effects.

pre_medications: Cyclizine 50mg up to three times a day oral when required (take home anti-emetic)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral
Allopurinol 300mg once a day for 7 days oral (cycle 1 only)
Thromboprophylaxis: dalteparin 5000 units SC OD or enoxaparin 40mg SC OD or heparin 5000 units SC BD (as per local formulary)
Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2mg after each loose stool to maximum 16mg/24 hours oral
Gastric protection: proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist in high-risk patients
Bisphosphonates in accordance with local policies
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
special_warnings: Thalidomide is highly teratogenic. All prescribers, patients and pharmacy staff must comply with the manufacturer's Pregnancy Prevention Programme. Prescriptions must be accompanied by a completed Prescription Authorisation Form.
Thalidomide associated with increased risk of thromboembolic events; thromboprophylaxis required for at least first 5 months, especially with additional thrombotic risk factors.

Panobinostat prolongs the QT interval. ECG prior to day 1; QTcF must be <480ms before starting panobinostat. Agents also associated with QT prolongation (ondansetron, metoclopramide, domperidone) should be used with caution.
Bortezomib given subcutaneously; SC route associated with lower incidence and severity of peripheral neuropathy.
Oral chemotherapy - NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. Short course oral chemotherapy must not be continued.
FBC checked prior to each bortezomib dose (days 1 and 8).
LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle.
Thalidomide should be taken at night to avoid daytime drowsiness.
All dose reductions due to adverse reactions should not be re-escalated without consultant approval. Third dose reduction: stop treatment.

drugs.notes: Days 1, 8, 15. Available as 50mg tablets. Swallow whole with a full glass of water. Dose modifications based on haematological parameters apply to cyclophosphamide only.
Days 1, 2, 3, 4 and 12, 13, 14, 15. Take in morning with or after food. If unable to tolerate standard dose, may reduce to 20mg or omit days 12-15.

Days 1-21 inclusive (continuous). Initiated at 100mg daily; increased by 50mg every 3 weeks if tolerated to target of 200mg. Available as 50mg capsules. Take at night. Oral chemotherapy - Pregnancy Prevention Programme mandatory. Prescriptions must be accompanied by Prescription Authorisation Form.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% of dose
Reduce, delay or omit cyclophosphamide until neutrophils are >=1x10^9/L and then either continue at full dose with growth factor support or give reduced dose and seek consultant advice
100% of dose
Reduce, delay or omit until platelets >=75x10^9/L and then continue at full dose or give reduced dose and seek consultant advice

dose_modifications.condition: Cyclophosphamide hepatic impairment - bilirubin >30 micromol/L or AST/ALT 2-3xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision. Evidence that exposure to active metabolites may not be increased, suggesting dose reduction may not be necessary.
100% of dose
75% of dose

dose_modifications.condition: Cyclophosphamide renal impairment - CrCl <10 ml/min or serum creatinine >300 micromol/L
dose_modifications.notes: Omit cyclophosphamide
May continue; if symptoms worsen consider dose reduction or interruption. May reintroduce at reduced dose if symptoms resolve.
Suspend treatment or reduce dose by at least 50%. May reintroduce at reduced dose if symptoms resolve to grade <=1.

dose_modifications.condition: Thalidomide peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Stop thalidomide treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Thalidomide - other NCI-CTC grade 3 toxicities or above
dose_modifications.notes: Stop thalidomide until recovery to NCI-CTC grade <=1. Cautious reintroduction at 50mg/day with dose escalation if tolerated.
Reduce to 20mg or omit days 12-15

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg up to three times a day oral when required (anti-emetic)
Ondansetron 8mg once a day on days 1, 8, 15 oral (take 15-30 minutes prior to cyclophosphamide administration)
Consider allopurinol 300mg once a day for 7 days oral (first cycle only)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral

Thromboprophylaxis: dalteparin 5000 units SC OD or enoxaparin 40mg SC OD or heparin 5000 units SC BD (as per local formulary); aspirin 75mg each morning is an alternative in very low risk patients once response obtained
Laxatives as required for thalidomide-induced constipation
Gastric protection: proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist in high-risk patients
Bisphosphonates in accordance with local policies
Mouthwashes according to local or national policy for mucositis
special_warnings: Thalidomide is highly teratogenic. All prescribers, patients and pharmacy staff must comply with the manufacturer's Pregnancy Prevention Programme. Prescriptions must be accompanied by a completed Prescription Authorisation Form.
Thalidomide associated with increased risk of thromboembolic events; thromboprophylaxis required for at least first 5 months, especially with additional thrombotic risk factors.

Pregnancy testing required: negative test must be obtained within 3 days of starting thalidomide; repeat every 4 weeks (every 2 weeks in women with irregular cycles); final test 4 weeks after last dose.
Thalidomide initiated at 100mg and increased by 50mg every 3 weeks if tolerated to target 200mg daily.
Oral chemotherapy - NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. Short course oral chemotherapy must not be continued.
Patients must be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. Paraprotein and/or light chains prior to each cycle.

Dose modifications based on haematological parameters apply to cyclophosphamide only. In previously untreated patients, myelosuppression prior to treatment likely reflects bone marrow infiltration; patients should receive at least first cycle with unmodified doses unless another cause found.
Cyclophosphamide tablets should be swallowed whole with a full glass of water.
Thalidomide should be taken at night to avoid daytime drowsiness.
Regular monitoring of blood glucose is considered good practice.
21 day cycle continued until maximal response (minimum 4 cycles), continued until plateau plus two cycles (8 cycles set in ARIA).
There are multiple versions of this protocol in use. Please ensure correct protocol is selected.
